stories.title: Knicks Set NBA Playoff Record — 47-Point Halftime Lead in 140-89 Demolition of Hawks

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
HISTORIC. The Knicks led by 47 at halftime — the largest halftime lead in NBA playoff history.

140-89 final. Series over. New York advances.

OG Anunoby: 29 pts (26 in the first half)
Mikal Bridges: 24 pts (10-for-12)
KAT: 12/11/10 triple-double

#Knicks

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
OG Anunoby dropped 26 points in the first half of an NBA playoff game last night.

The Knicks' 40-15 first quarter was the largest Q1 lead of the shot clock era.

New York is rolling into the second round. Scary.

#Knicks

facebook.long_form:
The New York Knicks didn't just win a basketball game Thursday night. They made history.

Leading 83-36 at halftime, the Knicks set the NBA playoff record for the largest halftime margin ever. Forty-seven points. The game was over by the end of the first quarter when New York led 40-15 — the largest first-quarter lead of the shot clock era.

OG Anunoby was absolutely unconscious in the first half, scoring 26 of his eventual 29 points before intermission, knocking down 4-for-4 from three-point range. Mikal Bridges was quietly brilliant, going 10-for-12 for 24 points. Karl-Anthony Towns capped it all with a triple-double — 12 points, 11 rebounds, 10 assists — in what became a celebration by halftime.

Final: 140-89. Knicks win series 4-2.

New York is heading to the Eastern Conference Semifinals, and whoever they face next is on notice. This team is not just talented — they're dangerous.
facebook.image_caption: New York Knicks — 47-point halftime lead. NBA playoff record. 140-89 final over the Hawks. This team is DANGEROUS. #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: Timberwolves Eliminate Nuggets Without Ant — McDaniels 32, Shannon 24 in 110-98 Win

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
The Timberwolves just eliminated the Nuggets WITHOUT:

❌ Anthony Edwards (knee)
❌ Donte DiVincenzo (Achilles)
❌ Ayo Dosunmu (calf)
❌ Kyle Anderson (illness)

Jaden McDaniels had 32. Terrence Shannon Jr. had 24.

Minnesota is BUILT DIFFERENT. #Timberwolves

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
2nd time in 3 years the Timberwolves have sent the Nuggets home.

Three straight Western Conference Semifinals for Minnesota.

And they did it missing their best player.

Next up: San Antonio. #Timberwolves #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Minnesota Timberwolves just pulled off one of the most impressive series clinchers you'll ever watch.

Without Anthony Edwards (left knee), Donte DiVincenzo (Achilles), Ayo Dosunmu (calf), and Kyle Anderson (illness), the Wolves eliminated the Denver Nuggets 110-98 in Game 6 to win the series 4-2. Four rotation players out. Still won.

Jaden McDaniels delivered 32 points, 4 rebounds, and 5 assists. Terrence Shannon Jr. added 24 points and 6 rebounds. Role players becoming heroes — that's the Minnesota Timberwolves in 2026.

This is the second time in three seasons that the Wolves have sent Denver packing. They're heading to their third straight Western Conference Semifinals, this time to face the San Antonio Spurs.

Anthony Edwards is listed week-to-week with a bone bruise and hyperextension. Minnesota has proven it can survive without him. But getting him back would make this team a genuine Western Conference Finals contender.
facebook.image_caption: Jaden McDaniels: 32 points. Terrence Shannon Jr.: 24 points. Anthony Edwards: not even on the court. The Timberwolves are UNREAL. Series over — Wolves win 4-2. #Timberwolves #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: Maxey 30 Forces Game 7 — 76ers Top Celtics 106-93 as Tatum Exits with Calf Issue

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Tyrese Maxey dropped 30. Joel Embiid had a near triple-double (19/10/8).

The Sixers are ALIVE. Game 7 is Saturday in Boston.

And Jayson Tatum left with a calf issue in the 3rd quarter.

#Sixers #Celtics #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Tatum post-game on the calf: "I wasn't overly concerned."

He left in the 3rd quarter while the Celtics trailed by double digits.

Game 7 is Saturday at TD Garden. Boston needs their star healthy.

#Celtics #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Down 3-1 to the defending champions. Written off. Left for dead.

Tyrese Maxey didn't read the script.

The Philadelphia 76ers dominated the Boston Celtics in Game 6, 106-93, forcing a winner-take-all Game 7 this Saturday at TD Garden. Maxey led the way with 30 points on 11-for-22 shooting, hitting 3-for-5 from three, 5-for-6 at the rim, and converting all 5 of his free throw attempts. Joel Embiid nearly had a triple-double: 19 points, 10 rebounds, and 8 assists. Paul George added 23.

But the story heading into Saturday isn't just Philadelphia's brilliance — it's Jayson Tatum's health. Boston's superstar left in the third quarter with left calf discomfort and finished with 17 points. Post-game, Tatum downplayed concerns: "I wasn't overly concerned," calling it tightness rather than structural damage. He expects to play Saturday.

Still, the timing is worth watching. Game 7. TD Garden. 7:30 PM ET Saturday. The Celtics need their star healthy, and the 76ers are riding the kind of momentum that makes anything possible.
facebook.image_caption: Tyrese Maxey: 30 points. Joel Embiid: 19/10/8. Jayson Tatum: exits in the 3rd quarter with a calf issue. Game 7 is Saturday in Boston. The NBA playoffs are INCREDIBLE. #Sixers #Celtics #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: Tonight's Triple-Header — Magic, Raptors, Rockets All Fight to Survive

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text:
THREE elimination games tonight — all on Prime Video:

🏀 Magic at Pistons, 7 PM ET (ORL leads 3-2)
🏀 Raptors at Cavaliers, 7:30 PM ET (CLE leads 3-2)
🏀 Rockets at Lakers, 9:30 PM ET (LAL leads 3-2)

One of the best nights of the NBA playoffs.

#NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text:
Reminder: The Magic are one win away from eliminating the No. 1 seed Pistons.

Tonight is their chance.

Detroit's playoff life is on the line at 7 PM ET on Prime.

#Magic #Pistons #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Three series. Three Game 6s. Three teams fighting to avoid elimination. One spectacular night of NBA basketball on Prime Video.

7:00 PM ET — Magic at Pistons (Orlando leads 3-2): The No. 8 seed Magic are one win away from knocking out Detroit's No. 1 seed. The Pistons rallied in Game 5 with an all-time duel — Cade Cunningham and Paolo Banchero each scored 45 — but Orlando has been the more consistent team. Can Detroit save its season at home?

7:30 PM ET — Raptors at Cavaliers (Cleveland leads 3-2): The road team has won every single game through five contests. Toronto heads to Cleveland with a chance to extend the series. Brandon Ingram is dealing with right heel inflammation. The Cavs want to end it at home tonight.

9:30 PM ET — Rockets at Lakers (L.A. leads 3-2): The Rockets shocked the Lakers in Game 5, 99-93, with Jabari Smith Jr. leading the way. Without Kevin Durant, Houston's young core has been fearless. LeBron James and the Lakers close at home tonight — but the Rockets are not backing down.

Three potential upsets. One massive Friday night.
facebook.image_caption: Game 6. Game 6. Game 6. Three series on the line tonight starting at 7 PM ET on Prime Video. Which teams survive? #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: Ant Edwards Week-to-Week — Inside the Injury and What It Means for the Wolves

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Anthony Edwards is week-to-week with a left knee bone bruise and hyperextension.

The Wolves still closed out the Nuggets — winning 2 straight without him.

Faces the Spurs next in the second round. Timeline unknown.

#Timberwolves #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Timberwolves may be celebrating their second-round berth, but a major question looms over their next series: when will Anthony Edwards return?

Edwards suffered a left knee hyperextension and bone bruise in Game 4 against the Nuggets. The team listed him week-to-week, and he missed both Games 5 and 6. Despite his absence — along with three other key rotation players — Minnesota won both games to close out Denver.

A bone bruise with hyperextension is one of the more unpredictable injuries in basketball. The encouraging news is there appears to be no structural ligament damage, which would be a far more serious issue. With rest and treatment, the hyperextension component often resolves within 1-2 weeks. The bone bruise typically takes a bit longer and causes lingering soreness even after swelling subsides.

The Wolves will face San Antonio in the Western Semis. Victor Wembanyama and De'Aaron Fox are rested and ready. Minnesota proved this week that they can compete without Edwards — Jaden McDaniels (32 pts) and Terrence Shannon Jr. (24 pts) filled the void brilliantly. But getting Ant back transforms this team from a competitive Round 2 participant to a legitimate Western Conference Finals threat.

The timeline remains unclear. Watch for official practice reports as the series approaches.
facebook.image_caption: Anthony Edwards: week-to-week with a left knee bone bruise. The Timberwolves went 2-0 without him to close out Denver. The big question: will he be ready for the Spurs? #Timberwolves #NBAPlayoffs
